# Aetas Workplace

> Aetas Workplace is an employee financial wellbeing partner for UK SMEs, LLPs and professional practices (10 to 250 employees). It eases the money worries that quietly drain focus, attendance and loyalty, through three things: a review of the benefits an employer already funds, practical financial education and resilience training for staff, and access to a hub of independent wellbeing partners. The commercial return is shown in plain pounds, a measure Aetas calls ROEI (Return on Employee Investment). Every engagement begins with a no-cost Workplace Review. Delivered by Matthew Steiner and the Aetas team, supported by sister firm Aetas Wealth (FCA registration 458421). These services are not regulated financial advice.

One philosophy, three firms: Aetas believes that better decisions create better futures. That belief runs through Aetas Wealth (personal financial planning), Aetas Workplace (employee financial wellbeing, measured by Return on Employee Investment, ROEI), and Aetas Impact (financial wellbeing and resilience for charities).

Aetas Workplace is not a self-serve benefits platform. It is a hands-on partner for smaller organisations, built around the employer's own people and constraints rather than a corporate model. Nothing is introduced without a Workplace Review first, and the return is always shown in plain pounds.

## Who this is for

- Business owners, managing directors, HR directors and finance directors in UK SMEs (10 to 250 employees) who want to ease the cost of financially stressed staff and see the return
- LLPs and professional practices, including law firms, accountancy practices, architecture and surveying firms, where financially stressed support staff cost fee-earner time and the fix must not add overhead to the partnership
- Employers who want a genuine, well-used benefit for their people, not a platform that sits unused

## What Aetas Workplace does

Where the Workplace Review shows financial wellbeing is a driver, a three-part programme addresses it, held together by ongoing support:

**Benefits review**, a review of pensions, benefits and financial support. Identifies gaps, inefficiencies and spend that is not performing.

**Education and resilience**, practical financial education and resilience training for staff. Group workshops and optional one-to-one guidance with a regulated financial planner.

**The wellbeing hub**, access, through independent partners, to a range of wellbeing and everyday-savings services in one place.

## The ROEI framework

ROEI (Return on Employee Investment) is how Aetas puts the return on better financial wellbeing into plain pounds. It considers salary, pension, benefits, recruitment, training, wellbeing and management time. The five stages are: Measure, Diagnose, Recommend, Implement, and Measure Again.

## Partners

- **Every Wellbeing**, Strategic Wellbeing Partner. Specialist workplace wellbeing: strategy, leadership and manager development, mental health, resilience and engagement.
- **Shop St**, Everyday Savings Partner. A cardless app giving staff discounts across more than 1,000 brands, from big retailers to local businesses, from as little as a penny per week.

## The team

- Matthew Steiner, Managing Director. Leads every Workplace Review personally. Over 20 years in financial services. matthew.steiner@aetas-wealth.com
- Daniel Cottam, Financial Planner. Delivers employee-facing workshops and one-to-one guidance sessions.
- Peter Rose APFS, Chartered Financial Planner and Pension Specialist. Specialist pension and financial planning support.

## The Aetas group

- **Aetas Workplace**, employee financial wellbeing for smaller employers
- **Aetas Wealth**, personal financial planning for directors, business owners and senior professionals
- **Aetas Impact**, financial wellbeing, governance and resilience for charities and purpose-led organisations

## Case studies

- [Bristol-based organisation](https://workplace.aetas-wealth.com/case-studies/bristol-organisation), pension review, workshops and partner services delivered. Ongoing engagement.
- [UK retail group](https://workplace.aetas-wealth.com/case-studies/uk-retail-group), diagnostic and benefits review completed. Shop St introduced as immediate employee benefit.

## Contact and booking

- Email: matthew.steiner@aetas-wealth.com
- [Book a Workplace Review](https://link.aetas-wealth.com/widget/form/9fkQAaigUnxLJQqOM17V), no cost, no obligation

## Regulatory

Aetas Workplace provides employee benefits, financial education and wellbeing services to employers. These services do not constitute regulated financial advice. Where regulated advice is required, this is provided separately by Aetas Wealth, a trading style of Insight Financial Associates Limited, authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA registration: 458421). Registered office: 13 Hanover Square, Mayfair, London W1S 1HN.


## Detailed guide summaries

### The Cost of Financial Anxiety in the Workplace
Examines the direct commercial cost of employee financial stress. Covers productivity loss (estimated at one hour per financially stressed employee per day), absence data, management time impact, and the business case for intervention. Includes employer action checklist.

### Building an Employee Benefits Strategy for SMEs
Practical guide for SME employers on constructing a benefits package that employees value and use. Covers pension governance, salary sacrifice structures, group risk insurance, and low-cost high-impact additions. Includes a prioritisation framework.

### Salary Sacrifice Pensions Explained for Employers
Plain-English explanation of how salary sacrifice pension arrangements work, the National Insurance savings available to both employer and employee, implementation steps, and common employer questions answered.

### Employee Benefits and National Insurance Planning
How employers can reduce their National Insurance liability through benefits design, salary sacrifice arrangements and efficient structuring of total reward. Relevant following Finance Act 2026 changes to employer NI thresholds.

### Measuring the ROI of Financial Wellbeing Programmes
How to measure the return on a financial wellbeing investment. Covers baseline measurement, defining success metrics, tracking productivity and retention improvements, and presenting the business case internally.

### Generational Differences in the Workforce
How different generations experience financial pressure differently, and what this means for benefits design, communication strategy and programme delivery. Covers Gen Z, Millennial, Gen X and Baby Boomer financial priorities and concerns.
